summary: Task 079 is complete. The shipped boundary in src/app/api/open/notes/route.ts and src/lib/external-note-api/auth.ts matches the documented static-key server-to-server contract: 503 when API_KEY is unset, 401 for missing/invalid X-API-Key, private-by-default create when isPublished is omitted, publish-on-create when isPublished is true, and publicUrl null for private notes. Docs are aligned across SECURITY, RELIABILITY, external-note-api spec, boundary design doc, public publishing spec, note enrichment spec, ARCHITECTURE.md, and both env examples. Regression coverage is substantive: unit tests protect disabled and auth-failure behavior, and tests/e2e/note-api.spec.ts covers private create, publish-on-create, missing key, invalid key, and request-shape rejection. The provided required command results for npm run test:e2e -- --grep @note-api and npm run verify are consistent with the inspected implementation.
satisfied_exit_criteria: The shipped docs describe the external note API contract explicitly, including one static API_KEY, X-API-Key auth, 503 disabled behavior, 401 auth failures, and private-by-default publish semantics.
Regression coverage protects private note create, publish-on-create, missing-key failure, invalid-key failure, and request-shape rejection.
The route fails closed when API_KEY is absent or wrong, and auth runs before request-body parsing or persistence.
npm run test:e2e -- --grep @note-api passed in the provided deterministic check output.
npm run verify passed in the provided deterministic check output.
